data.title: TWFG Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 beats by $0.01, revenue of $53.8M beats by $0.64M
data.description:
• TWFG press release (NASDAQ:TWFG): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $53.8M beats by $0.64M.
  •

data.title: Hapag-Lloyd sees 'huge surge' in volume, as China-to-U.S. bookings bounce after tariff truce
data.description:
Shipper Hapag-Lloyd (OTCPK:HPGLY) (OTCPK:HLAGF) +11.6% in Frankfurt trading Wednesday after reporting higher Q1 earnings and reiterating full-year guidance, while cautioning that global trade conflicts and continued disruptions to the flow of commerce could affect supply and demand in the container shipping industry this year.

The world's fifth-largest container line by capacity reported Q1 EBITDA of €1.01B (~$1.13B), up from €835M in the year-earlier quarter and in line with company guidance of ~€1B, while revenues rose 19% to €5.05B, as volumes and average freight rates both rose by nearly 9% Y/Y due to strong demand.

Hapag-Lloyd (OTCPK:HPGLY) (OTCPK:HLAGF) said it still expects EBITDA of 2.4B-3.9B in 2025, but adding that volatile freight rates and major geopolitical challenges mean guidance is subject to considerable uncertainty.

The shipping industry was handed a reprieve earlier this week after the U.S. and China agreed to lower their tariffs for at least 90 days while the two sides continue talks, but the company said it remains unclear when passage through the Red Sea will be safe again while U.S. import tariffs have added considerable uncertainty among customers, initially weighing on demand.

Hapag-Lloyd (OTCPK:HPGLY) (OTCPK:HLAGF) is seeing a "huge surge in volume" this week after the U.S. and China lowered tariffs, CEO Rolf Habben Jansen told Bloomberg, as the pause in the trade war has unleashed a wave of pent-up demand for transpacific shipping.

Jansen estimated the boost at more than 50% compared with recent weeks, with bookings from China to the U.S., which had been down sharply during the past few weeks, rebounding strongly with other regions "all remaining pretty stable."

Major shipping lines including Hapag-Lloyd (OTCPK:HPGLY) (OTCPK:HLAGF) have avoided sailing through the Red Sea since late 2023, and Jansen said companies "need visibility that it is safe and that it is going to remain safe for a longer period of time, because what we want to avoid at all costs is to go in and out."

data.title: B. Riley Financial unable to timely file quartery report on Form 10-Q
data.description:
B. Riley Financial (NASDAQ:RILY) said in a filing on Tuesday that it was unable to timely file its financial report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended March 31.

The required filing date was May 12.

The company said it is working to complete the audit and finalize the annual report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024.

B. Riley had said in November last year that it believes it can return to a normal process of filing financial reports starting in 2025.

data.title: Main Street Capital falls after short pitch at Sohn Investment Conf
data.description:
• Victor Bonilla, founder of Jehoshaphat Research, recommends shorting stock of Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) because a dividend cut is likely.
  • He presented his idea Wednesday at the Sohn Investment Conference in New York.
  • Main Street didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment. 
  • Shares of Main Street (NYSE:MAIN) fell 2.7%.

--reported by Rob Williams in New York.

data.title: Take-Two Q4 Earnings Preview: All Eyes on Grand Theft Auto VI
data.description:
Take-Two (NASDAQ:TTWO) is set to announce fourth quarter earnings on Thursday, and investors will watch out for the company’s net booking guidance for FY 2026 and updates on Grand Theft Auto VI.

Wall Street expects the video game maker to post EPS of $1.09, while revenue is expected to increase 10.7% Y/Y to $1.55 billion during the quarter.

The company forecasted net bookings for Q4 2025 to range from $1.48 billion to $1.58 billion, and said it expects record level of net bookings for fiscal 2026 and 2027 driven by Grand Theft Auto VI.

Rockstar Games, a Take-Two (NASDAQ:TTWO) game publishing unit, has uploaded a second trailer for Grand Theft Auto VI, days after announcing the delay of the release of the company's next blockbuster title.

Since its release in the nineties, Take-Two’s GTA has become one of the biggest franchises in the gaming world and has garnered millions of followers.

BofA reiterated its Buy rating on TTWO due to the reduced risk of another delay, and the greater likelihood that GTA 6 could launch with a price tag of over $70.

Jefferies also remained bullish about TTWO and increased its price target to $270 from $225.  The brokerage firm also updated its scenarios for GTA VI and moved base case FY27 GTA to 50 million console units, $4.2 billion total GTA bookings, and TTWO FY27 EPS of $10.58.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating System rated TTWO as a Strong Buy, with A+ rating in terms of growth and A rating in prospects of momentum.  Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts are also bullish and rated the stock as Buy.

“Take-Two's robust release pipeline, including Civilization VII and Borderlands 4, supports management's forecast of record net bookings in FY 2026 and 2027,” argued Seeking Alpha analyst Simple Investment Ideas.

Over the last one year, TTWO has beaten EPS estimates 0% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have not seen any upward revisions, compared to two downward moves. Revenue estimates have seen two upward revisions versus no downward moves.

Shares of TTWO gained over 23% so far this year, outperforming the broader S&P 500 Index, which was flat during the same period.

data.title: Xenia announces additional $100M share repurchase authorization
data.description:
REIT Xenia Hotels & Resorts (NYSE:XHR) announced on Wednesday the authorization of up to an additional $100M share repurchase program.

The buybacks may be made in the open market and through privately negotiated transactions, or by other means.

As of May 13, the company had about $180M remaining under its total share repurchase authorization.

Additionally, Xenia Hotels declared $0.14/share quarterly dividend, in line with the previous.

data.title: AbbVie and ADARx partner to develop RNA-based therapies
data.description:
• AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) and ADARx Pharmaceuticals announced on Wednesday a collaboration and license option agreement to develop small interfering RNA therapies for several disease areas, including neuroscience, immunology, and cancer.
  • As part of the deal, AbbVie will pay ADARx $335M upfront.
  • ADARx will also be eligible for billions more in milestone payments, option fees, and royalties based on future progress and sales.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow continue look for direction after softer inflation report
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages look for direction on Wednesday, a day after the S&P and the Nasdaq closed higher, as soft inflation data soothed sentiments.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was near flat, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis point to 4.49%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.02%.

Wall Street's benchmark S&P 500 index and tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite gauge on Tuesday extended a massive rally from the previous session, as sentiment was boosted by soft inflation data. The blue-chip Dow bucked the trend, weighed down by a plunge in component UnitedHealth.

“It's been another 24 hours where the Trump administration continues to hog the headlines. With the president in the Middle East, various stories on AI supported a huge tech-led rally...that got a further boost thanks to a softer-than-expected CPI print,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

On Tuesday, the headline and core consumer prices rose +0.2% M/M, below the +0.3% consensus. On an annual basis, headline CPI came in at +2.3%, also below the +2.4% estimate, while core CPI was in line at +2.8%. 

“Our U.S. economists think the April data is still too early for the Liberation Day tariffs to show up in the aggregate numbers, and they don’t expect the effects to show up in consumer prices until June,” Reid added.

U.S. consumer price inflation did not show many trade tax consequences. The slowing of tourism-related prices might reflect how visitors are wary of entering the U.S., but complications in timing Easter’s effect are also relevant. Appliance price inflation is a concern, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The economic calendar is light today. The wholesale inflation data is expected to land on Thursday.

data.title: Comerica's chief risk officer Brian Goldman resigns
data.description:
Diversified lender Comerica (NYSE:CMA) said in a filing on Wednesday that Brian Goldman has resigned as senior executive vice president and chief risk officer, effective May 23.

Goldman is set to "pursue another opportunity".

The company said Goldman's resignation was not the result of any disagreement with Comerica or the board.

Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Credit Officer  Melinda Chausse will serve in the additional role of chief risk officer on an interim basis.

data.title: nCino gains amid long pitch at Sohn Conference
data.description:
nCino (NASDAQ:NCNO) edged higher by 0.8% as it was recommended at the Sohn Investment Conference in New York.

Connie Lee, founder and chief investment officer at startup hedge fund Felis Advantage, recommends buying nCino (NASDAQ:NCNO). Lee expects nCino (CNCO) shares to gain 2.5 times in next three years. 

Kristov Paulus, founder, managing partner, and chief investment officer at Kultura Capital Management, which was started in February 2024, recommends buying stock in the brokerage Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD). 
 
Robinhood is popular among younger investors, with 75% of its customers belonging to the millennial and Gen Z age cohorts, compared with 14% for the more established Charles Schwab, according to Paulus. Shares of Robinhood rose 1%.
 
Alexandra Engler, managing partner at Arene Capital, which focuses on "idiosyncratic dislocations" in stock values, recommends buying shares of Celanese (NYSE:CE). She said negative news flow is close to an end, and its acetyls business is poised for growth.   
 
--reported by Rob Williams in New York.
